evidence.velocity_vs_rigour: improve data governance through clear definitions and reusable logic; governed reporting environments
evidence.velocity_vs_rigour_explanation: Classified as rigour because responsibilities centre on data governance, clear definitions, reusable logic, and governed reporting environments — with no velocity or speed-to-ship signals.
evidence.domain_risk_explanation: Classified as moderate because Too Good To Go is a consumer tech marketplace where errors affect business scaling decisions without regulatory or direct financial reporting consequence.
evidence.data_team_maturity: designing the reporting architecture, semantic models and data foundations; Data Engineering as named distinct team
evidence.data_team_maturity_explanation: Classified as mid because Data Engineering is a named distinct partner team and the role is building out the reporting architecture within an existing data function.
evidence.jd_authorship: Design performant LookML models, Explores and dashboards; replace manual reporting and Google Sheets processes with scalable solutions
evidence.jd_authorship_explanation: Classified as hiring_manager because specific tools (LookML, Explores) and concrete problems (replacing Google Sheets) are named in responsibilities — reader understands what this role involves on a Tuesday morning.
evidence.stakeholder_orientation: designing the reporting architecture, semantic models and data foundations; own technical design of BI products
evidence.stakeholder_orientation_explanation: Classified as internal_data because the primary output is the BI platform itself — semantic models, reporting architecture, and data foundations for internal data consumers (Parcels Planning team).
evidence.autonomy_level: own technical design and development of BI products and semantic models; designing the reporting architecture
evidence.autonomy_level_explanation: Classified as strategic because 'own technical design' and 'designing the reporting architecture' signal the candidate defines the BI foundation, not just executes against a spec.
evidence.ai_role_explanation: No AI tool usage or AI infrastructure enablement is mentioned anywhere in the JD.
evidence.testing_framing: improve data governance through clear definitions and reusable logic
evidence.testing_framing_explanation: Classified as responsibility because 'improve data governance through clear definitions and reusable logic' frames governance/quality as an owned candidate responsibility using an action verb.
evidence.loss_aversion_framing: data governance through clear definitions; governed reporting environments
evidence.loss_aversion_framing_explanation: Classified as moderate because governance and clear metric definitions signal concern about data inconsistency, but the framing is delivery-oriented for a 12-month platform build, not compliance or trust-failure dominated.
evidence.greenfield_vs_fix: replace manual reporting and Google Sheets processes with scalable solutions; designing the reporting architecture
evidence.greenfield_vs_fix_explanation: Classified as mixed because the role involves both designing a new reporting architecture (greenfield) and replacing existing manual processes (fix_scale).
evidence.loss_aversion: improve data governance through clear definitions and reusable logic — governance and consistency are named concerns but secondary to the platform delivery mission.
